title: Investigative Journalist
description: Investigative journalists uncover hidden truths and expose wrongdoing through in-depth research, analysis, and reporting. They play a crucial role in holding power accountable and informing the public on important issues.
content: Investigative journalists require strong research, interviewing, and writing skills. They often work on long-term projects, delving into complex topics such as corruption, human rights abuses, and environmental issues. This career demands persistence, ethical integrity, and the ability to handle pressure and potential risks.

financialFeasibility.note: While journalism is accessible across socio-economic backgrounds, pursuing advanced degrees or working for prestigious organizations may require financial support. Freelancing can be a viable option for those with limited resources.

sectorScore.remarks: Opportunities exist in both government-funded and private media organizations, as well as independent online platforms. Freelancing is also common.

relatedCareers.reason: Analyzes political events and policies, often contributing to journalistic reports.
relatedCareers.title: Political Analyst

relatedCareers.reason: Advocates for human rights, often working with journalists to expose abuses.
relatedCareers.title: Human Rights Lawyer

relatedCareers.reason: Creates films that investigate and expose social and political issues.
relatedCareers.title: Documentary Filmmaker

genderDistribution.note: While historically male-dominated, the field is becoming increasingly diverse, with more women taking on prominent investigative roles.
